reason: Gap-fix is INCOMPLETE: missing dependency edge 1.2.1 -> 1.1 (still no Depends-on line; 'af get 1.2.1' confirms zero declared deps). Node 1.2.1's load-bearing FIRST inference 'by node 1.1, 1 in A=Im P' directly consumes node 1.1's validated result, but 1.1 is an UNCLE (sibling of parent 1.2), not an ancestor, so it is outside 1.2.1's declared proof scope; a parent's 'Depends on: 1.1' does NOT transitively place 1.1 in the scope of 1.2's children. This is the af checklist BAD example 'uses result from node X but X not listed as dependency'. The validated siblings set the bar (1.2 'Depends on: 1.1'; 1.3 'Depends on: 1.1, 1.2'; 1.4 'Depends on: 1.1, 1.2, 1.3'). The MATH is sound and every GT-* byte-verifies in refs/hos/joa-m.md: GT-orderunit-def (line 368) verbatim 'An element e in A^+ is called an order unit for A ...' makes 'e in A^+' definitional; GT-jb-orderunit-3310 (line 2552) verbatim 'order unit the identity 1'; so 1 in V_+, and with 1 in A (1.1) + def-near-fixed-algebra (A_+=A cap V_+), 1 in A_+. Conclusion TRUE but proof STRUCTURE ungrounded. FIX: declare node 1.1 as a dependency of 1.2.1, then validate; AND declare 1.2.1 as a dependency of parent 1.2 (currently 1.2 lists only 'Depends on: 1.1', so clause (i) rests on an undeclared, still-pending sub-node). Root 1 cannot be accepted while 1.2/1.2.1 remain pending with these open dependency gaps.
